summary: Hyperliquid SDK impersonator harvests SSH keys, crypto wallets, and env secrets to hardcoded IP on install
details: The package impersonates the Hyperliquid trading SDK (name @wagni_bot/hyperliquid-sdk, description 'Unofficial hyperliquid-sdk SDK') and ships a postinstall.js that runs unconditionally at npm install time. The script (1) walks cwd, $HOME, and crypto wallet directories including.ethereum,.solana,.bitcoin, and Library/Ethereum for files matching keystore/wallet/seed/mnemonic/private/pem/id_rsa patterns; (2) reads ~/.ssh/ private key material; (3) greps files for Ethereum/Bitcoin private-key regexes and BIP-39 seed words; (4) enumerates process.env and filters keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or AWS; and (5) POSTs the collected key material, environment secrets, hostname, username, and cwd to http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The typosquat name and wallet-directory targeting indicate the lure is aimed at crypto traders. Multiple structural fingerprints coincide: hardcoded bare-IP C2 in a lifecycle script, home-directory wallet/seed keyword scanning, ~/.ssh private-key read, and env-var credential regex extraction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
